member.name: T:Transaction
member.summary: <summary> Base class for all transactions that may be built and submitted to Hedera. </summary> <param name="<T>">The type of the transaction. Used to enable chaining.</param>

member.name: T:Transaction_2
member.summary: The maximum transaction fee the client is willing to pay

member.name: M:Transaction.#ctor
member.summary: Should the transaction id be regenerated

member.name: M:Transaction.#ctor_2
member.summary: Constructor.

member.name: M:Transaction.#ctor(Proto.TransactionBody)
member.summary: This constructor is used to construct from a scheduled transaction body

member.name: M:Transaction.#ctor(DictionaryLinked{TransactionId,DictionaryLinked{AccountId,Proto.Transaction}})
member.summary: This constructor is used to construct via fromBytes

member.name: M:Transaction.RequireNotFrozen
member.summary: The maximum transaction fee the operator (paying account) is willing to pay.

member.name: M:Transaction.RequireNotFrozen_2
member.summary: Extract the memo for the transaction.

member.name: M:Transaction.RequireNotFrozen_3
member.summary:
Sets the duration that this transaction is valid for.

This is defaulted by the SDK to 120 seconds (or two minutes).

member.name: P:Transaction.SourceTransactionBody
member.summary: Transaction constructors end their work by setting sourceTransactionBody. The expectation is that the Transaction subclass constructor will pick up where the Transaction superclass constructor left off, and will unpack the data in the transaction body.

member.name: P:Transaction.FrozenBodyBuilder
member.summary: The builder that gets re-used to build each outer transaction. freezeWith() will create the frozenBodyBuilder. The presence of frozenBodyBuilder indicates that this transaction is frozen.

member.name: M:Transaction.RequireNotFrozen_4
member.summary: The key that will sign the batch of which this Transaction is a part of.

member.name: P:Transaction.ShouldRegenerateTransactionId
member.summary: Should the transaction id be regenerated.
member.returns: should the transaction id be regenerated

member.name: T:Transaction_3
member.summary:
Set the ID for this transaction.

The transaction ID includes the operator's account ( the account paying the transaction fee). If two transactions have the same transaction ID, they won't both have an effect. One will complete normally and the other will fail with a duplicate transaction status.

Normally, you should not use this method. Just before a transaction is executed, a transaction ID will be generated from the operator on the client.

member.name: P:Transaction.OuterTransactions
member.summary: An SDK [Transaction] is composed of multiple, raw protobuf transactions. These should be functionally identical, except pointing to different nodes. When retrying a transaction after a network error or retry-able status response, we try a different transaction and thus a different node.

member.name: P:Transaction.InnerSignedTransactions
member.summary: An SDK [Transaction] is composed of multiple, raw protobuf transactions. These should be functionally identical, except pointing to different nodes. When retrying a transaction after a network error or retry-able status response, we try a different transaction and thus a different node.

member.name: P:Transaction.SigPairLists
member.summary: A set of signatures corresponding to every unique public key used to sign the transaction.

member.name: P:Transaction.TransactionIds
member.summary: List of IDs for the transaction based on the operator because the transaction ID includes the operator's account

member.name: P:Transaction.PublicKeys
member.summary: publicKeys and signers are parallel Array. If the signer associated with a public key is null, that means that the private key associated with that public key has already contributed a signature to sigPairListBuilders, but the signer is not available (likely because this came from fromBytes())

member.name: M:Transaction.freezeWith(Client)
member.summary: publicKeys and signers are parallel Array. If the signer associated with a public key is null, that means that the private key associated with that public key has already contributed a signature to sigPairListBuilders, but the signer is not available (likely because this came from fromBytes())

member.name: M:Transaction.OnFreeze(Proto.TransactionBody)
member.summary: Called in {@link #freezeWith(Client)} just before the transaction body is built. The intent is for the derived class to assign their data variant to the transaction body.

member.name: M:Transaction.OnScheduled(Proto.SchedulableTransactionBody)
member.summary: Called in {@link #schedule()} when converting transaction into a scheduled version.

member.name: M:Transaction.DoSchedule(Proto.TransactionBody)
member.summary: Converts transaction into a scheduled version

member.name: M:Transaction.KeyAlreadySigned(PublicKey)
member.summary: Checks if a public key is already added to the transaction
member.returns: if the public key is already added

member.name: M:Transaction.RequireNotFrozen_5
member.summary: Throw an exception if the transaction is frozen.

member.name: M:Transaction.RequireOneNodeAccountId
member.summary: Throw an exception if there is not exactly one node id set.

member.name: M:Transaction.AddSignature(PublicKey,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Add a signature to the transaction.

member.name: M:Transaction.AddSignature(PublicKey,System.Byte[],TransactionId,AccountId)
member.summary: Adds a signature to the transaction for a specific transaction id and node id. This is useful for signing chunked transactions like FileAppendTransaction, since they can have multiple transaction ids.
member.param: The public key to add signature for
The specific transaction ID to match
The specific node ID to match
member.exception: if unmarshaling fails or invalid signed transaction

member.name: M:Transaction.BuildAllTransactions
member.summary: Build all the transactions.

member.name: M:Transaction.BuildTransaction(System.Int32)
member.summary: Will build the specific transaction at {@code index} This function is only ever called after the transaction is frozen.
member.param: the index of the transaction to be built

member.name: M:Transaction.Freeze
member.summary: Freeze this transaction from further modification to prepare for signing or serialization.

member.name: M:Transaction.FreezeWith(Client)
member.summary:
Freeze this transaction from further modification to prepare for signing or serialization.

Will use the `Client`, if available, to generate a default Transaction ID and select 1/3 nodes to prepare this transaction for.

the configured client {@code this}

member.name: M:Transaction.GetRequiredChunks
member.summary: There must be at least one chunk.

member.name: M:Transaction.GetSignatures
member.summary: Extract list of account id and public keys.
member.returns: the list of account id and public keys

member.name: M:Transaction.GetSignableNodeBodyBytesList
member.summary: Returns a list of SignableNodeTransactionBodyBytes objects for each signed transaction in the transaction list. The NodeID represents the node that this transaction is signed for. The TransactionID is useful for signing chunked transactions like FileAppendTransaction, since they can have multiple transaction ids.
member.exception: if transaction is not frozen or protobuf parsing fails

member.name: M:Transaction.GetTransactionSize
member.summary: This method retrieves the size of the transaction

member.name: M:Transaction.GetTransactionBodySize
member.summary: This method retrieves the transaction body size

member.name: M:Transaction.GetTransactionHash
member.summary: Extract a byte array of the transaction hash.

member.name: M:Transaction.GetTransactionHashPerNode
member.summary: Extract the list of account id and hash records.
member.returns: the list of account id and hash records

member.name: M:Transaction.GenerateTransactionIds(TransactionId,System.Int32)
member.summary: Generate transaction id's.
member.param: the number of id's to generate.

member.name: M:Transaction.IsFrozen
member.summary: Check if transaction is frozen.

member.name: M:Transaction.Schedule
member.summary: Extract the scheduled transaction.

member.name: M:Transaction.SignTransaction(System.Int32)
member.summary: Will sign the specific transaction at {@code index} This function is only ever called after the transaction is frozen.
member.param: the index of the transaction to sign

member.name: M:Transaction.SignWithOperator(Client)
member.summary: Sign the transaction with the configured client.

member.name: M:Transaction.SignWith(PublicKey,System.Func{System.Byte[],System.Byte[]})
member.summary: Sign the transaction.

member.name: M:Transaction.ToBytes
member.summary: Extract a byte array representation.

member.name: M:Transaction.WipeTransactionLists(System.Int32)
member.summary: Wipe / reset the transaction list.

member.name: M:Transaction.SetNodeAccountIds(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{AccountId})
member.summary:
Set the account IDs of the nodes that this transaction will be submitted to.

Providing an explicit node account ID interferes with client-side load balancing of the network. By default, the SDK will pre-generate a transaction for 1/3 of the nodes on the network. If a node is down, busy, or otherwise reports a fatal error, the SDK will try again with a different node.

The list of node AccountIds to be set {@code this}

member.name: M:Transaction.Batchify(Client,Key)
member.summary: batchify method is used to mark a transaction as part of a batch transaction or make it so-called inner transaction. The Transaction will be frozen and signed by the operator of the client.

member.name: M:Transaction.Sign(PrivateKey)
member.summary: Sign the transaction.

member.name: M:Transaction.AddSignatureIfNotExists(System.Int32,PublicKey,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Adds signature if it doesn't already exist for the given public key.
member.returns: true if signature was added, false if it already existed

member.name: M:Transaction.IsSignatureAlreadyPresent(Proto.SignatureMap,PublicKey)
member.summary: Checks if a signature for the given public key already exists.
member.returns: true if signature already exists, false otherwise

member.name: M:Transaction.MatchesTargetTransactionAndNode(Proto.TransactionBody,TransactionId,AccountId)
member.summary: Checks if the transaction body matches the target transaction ID and node ID.
member.param: The target transaction ID to match against
The target node ID to match against
member.returns: true if both the transaction ID and node ID match the targets, false otherwise

member.name: M:Transaction.ProcessedSignatureForTransaction(System.Int32,PublicKey,System.Byte[],TransactionId,AccountId)
member.summary: Processes signature addition for a single transaction at the given index.
member.param: The index of the transaction to process
The public key to add signature for
The specific transaction ID to match
The specific node ID to match
member.returns: true if signature was added, false otherwise

member.name: M:Transaction.UpdateTransactionState(PublicKey)
member.summary: Updates the transaction state after adding a signature.
member.param: The public key that was added
